content_of_post_to_answer: Is the mind identical to the brain, or is the brain identical to the mind to Rene Descartes?
answer: For Descartes, the mind and the brain are not identical. In fact, he distinguished between mind and body altogether. The mind is a thinking, non-physical substance; the brain is part of the physical body. What makes Descartes interesting is that he wasn't just saying they are different things—he was arguing that you can doubt the existence of your body, but you cannot doubt that you are thinking. For him, consciousness was more certain than any physical object, including the brain.
